---
title: "511 Exploration Data Analysis"
date: "2026-05-03"
output:
  pdf_document:
    latex_engine: xelatex
  html_document:
    df_print: paged
---

# =============================================================================
# EDA Data Cleaning
# =============================================================================

```{r source}
# =============================================================================
# Source: https://trails-wa-rco.hub.arcgis.com

#  df_q1  |  COMPARATIVE  | surface type vs. segment length & width by use
#  df_q2  |  CORRELATION  | trail width vs. number of permitted use types
#  df_q3  |  GEOGRAPHIC   | motorized vs. non-motorized trail miles by county
#  df_q4  |  TIME/TREND   | construction volume over time by trail type & agency
#
# Variable inventory per sub-dataset:
# ┌──────┬──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
# │ df_q1│ objectid, trail_name, surface_cat, use_cat,                     │
# │      │ segment_length_miles, trail_width                                │
# ├──────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
# │ df_q2│ objectid, trail_name, trail_width, num_uses,                    │
# │      │ motor_type, [13 binary use flags]                                │
# ├──────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
# │ df_q3│ objectid, trail_name, county, region,                           │
# │      │ is_motorized, segment_length_miles                               │
# ├──────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
# │ df_q4│ objectid, trail_name, year_constructed, decade, era,            │
# │      │ trail_type_cat, agency_clean, segment_length_miles              │
# └──────┴──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
# =============================================================================
```




```{r s0}
# ── 0. LIBRARIES ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
library(tidyverse)
library(janitor)    
library(skimr)       
```


```{r s1}
# ── 1. LOAD & INSPECT ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

trails_raw <- read_csv("trails.csv", show_col_types = FALSE)
trails     <- trails_raw |> clean_names()   # → snake_case column names

cat("Raw:", nrow(trails), "rows x", ncol(trails), "cols\n")
glimpse(trails)
skim(trails)
```




```{r s2}
# ── 2. SHARED BASE CLEANING ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
#
#  Rules applied here affect every sub-dataset equally:
#    a) Trim whitespace in all character columns
#    b) Normalize placeholder strings to NA
#    c) Keep only active / existing trail segments

trails_base <- trails |>
  
  # whitespace
  mutate(across(where(is.character), str_squish)) |>
  
  # Blank / placeholder -> NA
  mutate(across(where(is.character), ~ na_if(., ""))) |>
  mutate(across(where(is.character), ~ na_if(., "N/A"))) |>
  mutate(across(where(is.character), ~ na_if(., "n/a"))) |>
  mutate(across(where(is.character), ~ na_if(., "None"))) |>
  mutate(across(where(is.character), ~ na_if(., "Unknown"))) |>
  mutate(across(where(is.character), ~ na_if(., "Not Specified"))) |>
  
  # Active trails only (remove proposed / decommissioned / closed)
  filter(is.na(trail_status) |
           str_to_lower(trail_status) %in% c("existing", "open"))

cat("Base after global clean:", nrow(trails_base), "rows\n")
```




```{r q1}
# =============================================================================
# SUB-DATASET 1 — COMPARATIVE
# Question: How do trail surface types compare across primary use categories
#           in terms of average segment length and trail width?
#
# Variables:
#   objectid             — unique row ID
#   trail_name           — for reference / labeling
#   surface_cat          — derived: broad surface group (Paved, Gravel, etc.)
#   use_cat              — derived: broad primary use group (Hiking, Biking, etc.)
#   segment_length_miles — continuous outcome variable (length comparison)
#   trail_width          — continuous outcome variable (width comparison)
# =============================================================================

df_q1 <- trails_base |>
  
  # 1. Keep rows with all four required raw variables
  filter(
    !is.na(trail_surface),
    !is.na(primary_use),
    !is.na(segment_length_miles),
    !is.na(trail_width)
  ) |>
  
  # 2. Remove physically impossible / extreme lengths
  #    negative length (-1.0 min in raw) = data entry error
  #    > 50 miles = almost certainly a cumulative route total, not one segment
  filter(segment_length_miles > 0, segment_length_miles <= 50) |>
  
  # 3. Remove zero-width trails (unrecorded, not a real trail width)
  filter(trail_width > 0) |>
  
  # 4. Collapse trail_surface into surface_cat (6 broad groups)
  mutate(surface_cat = case_when(
    str_detect(str_to_lower(trail_surface), "paved|asphalt|concrete")    ~ "Paved",
    str_detect(str_to_lower(trail_surface), "gravel|crushed")            ~ "Gravel/Crushed",
    str_detect(str_to_lower(trail_surface), "native|dirt|natural|earth") ~ "Native/Dirt",
    str_detect(str_to_lower(trail_surface), "wood|boardwalk")            ~ "Wood/Boardwalk",
    str_detect(str_to_lower(trail_surface), "snow|ice")                  ~ "Snow/Ice",
    TRUE                                                                  ~ "Other"
  )) |>
  
  # 5. Collapse primary_use into use_cat (6 broad groups)
  mutate(use_cat = case_when(
    str_detect(str_to_lower(primary_use), "hik|walk|foot")               ~ "Hiking/Walking",
    str_detect(str_to_lower(primary_use), "bik|cycl|mountain bike")      ~ "Biking",
    str_detect(str_to_lower(primary_use), "atv|ohv|motoriz|4wd|jeep")    ~ "Motorized",
    str_detect(str_to_lower(primary_use), "horse|equestrian|pack")       ~ "Equestrian",
    str_detect(str_to_lower(primary_use), "ski|snow|snowshoe")           ~ "Winter Use",
    str_detect(str_to_lower(primary_use), "water|canoe|kayak")           ~ "Water",
    TRUE                                                                  ~ "Other/Multi"
  )) |>
  
  # 6. Keep only the 6 variables needed for Q1
  select(
    objectid,
    trail_name,
    surface_cat,            # grouping variable (x-axis / facet)
    use_cat,                # grouping variable (color / facet)
    segment_length_miles,   # outcome 1
    trail_width             # outcome 2
  )

cat("\n-- df_q1 (Comparative) --\n")
cat("Rows:", nrow(df_q1), "| Cols:", ncol(df_q1), "\n")
cat("Columns:", paste(names(df_q1), collapse = ", "), "\n")
tabyl(df_q1, surface_cat) |> print()
tabyl(df_q1, use_cat)     |> print()

```




```{r q2}
# =============================================================================
# SUB-DATASET 2 — CORRELATION
# Question: Does trail width correlate with the number of permitted use types?
#
# Variables:
#   objectid        — unique row ID
#   trail_name      — for reference
#   trail_width     — continuous predictor (x-axis)
#   num_uses        — derived: count of permitted use types (y-axis)
#   motor_type      — derived: Motorized Only / Non-Motorized Only / Mixed
#   [13 use flags]  — binary 0/1 columns kept for per-use breakdown charts
# =============================================================================

# All 13 use-type columns present in the raw data (raw values: "Yes" / "No" / NA)
use_cols <- c(
  "hiking_walking", "bicycle", "e_bike",
  "x4wd", "atv", "motorcycle",
  "pack_and_saddle",
  "motorized_watercraft", "non_motorized_watercraft",
  "cross_country_ski", "snowmobile", "snowshoe", "dog_sled"
)

df_q2 <- trails_base |>
  
  # 1. Need a valid, non-zero trail width
  filter(!is.na(trail_width), trail_width > 0) |>
  
  # 2. Binarize all use-type columns: "Yes" = 1, everything else = 0
  #    NA is treated as 0 (not recorded = not permitted for this analysis)
  mutate(across(
    all_of(use_cols),
    ~ if_else(str_to_lower(.) == "yes", 1L, 0L, missing = 0L)
  )) |>
  
  # 3. Derive num_uses = total number of permitted use types per segment
  mutate(num_uses = rowSums(across(all_of(use_cols)), na.rm = TRUE)) |>
  
  # 4. Drop segments with zero uses recorded (nothing to correlate against)
  filter(num_uses > 0) |>
  
  # 5. Classify each segment as Motorized / Non-Motorized / Mixed
  mutate(
    has_motorized    = (x4wd + atv + motorcycle + snowmobile) > 0,
    has_nonmotorized = (hiking_walking + bicycle + cross_country_ski +
                          snowshoe + pack_and_saddle) > 0,
    motor_type = case_when(
      has_motorized & !has_nonmotorized  ~ "Motorized Only",
      !has_motorized & has_nonmotorized  ~ "Non-Motorized Only",
      has_motorized & has_nonmotorized   ~ "Mixed",
      TRUE                               ~ "Unclassified"
    )
  ) |>
  
  # 6. Keep only the variables needed for Q2
  select(
    objectid,
    trail_name,
    trail_width,          # continuous predictor
    num_uses,             # derived count — response variable
    motor_type,           # color / facet grouping
    all_of(use_cols)      # individual flags for breakdown analysis
  )

cat("\n-- df_q2 (Correlation) --\n")
cat("Rows:", nrow(df_q2), "| Cols:", ncol(df_q2), "\n")
cat("Columns:", paste(names(df_q2), collapse = ", "), "\n")
summary(df_q2$trail_width) |> print()
summary(df_q2$num_uses)    |> print()
tabyl(df_q2, motor_type)   |> print()
```


```{r q3}
# =============================================================================
# SUB-DATASET 3 — GEOGRAPHIC
# Question: Which WA counties have the highest density of motorized vs.
#           non-motorized trails, and are motorized trails concentrated
#           in Eastern WA?
#
# Variables:
#   objectid             — unique row ID
#   trail_name           — for reference
#   county               — cleaned county name (geographic grouping unit)
#   region               — derived: "Eastern WA" or "Western WA"
#   is_motorized         — derived: "Motorized" or "Non-Motorized"
#   segment_length_miles — for aggregating total trail miles per county
# =============================================================================

# 20 counties east of the Cascades
eastern_wa_counties <- c(
  "Adams", "Asotin", "Benton", "Chelan", "Columbia", "Douglas",
  "Ferry", "Franklin", "Garfield", "Grant", "Kittitas", "Klickitat",
  "Lincoln", "Okanogan", "Pend Oreille", "Spokane", "Stevens",
  "Walla Walla", "Whitman", "Yakima"
)

df_q3 <- trails_base |>
  
  # 1. Need county and a valid segment length
  filter(!is.na(county), !is.na(segment_length_miles)) |>
  filter(segment_length_miles > 0, segment_length_miles <= 50) |>
  
  # 2. Standardize county names
  #    Remove trailing "County" suffix, title-case, trim spaces
  #    e.g. "king county" -> "King",  "PIERCE COUNTY" -> "Pierce"
  mutate(county = str_remove(county, regex("\\s*county$", ignore_case = TRUE)) |>
           str_to_title() |>
           str_squish()) |>
  
  # 3. Binarize use columns to classify motorized vs. non-motorized
  mutate(across(
    all_of(use_cols),
    ~ if_else(str_to_lower(.) == "yes", 1L, 0L, missing = 0L)
  )) |>
  mutate(
    is_motorized = if_else(
      (atv + motorcycle + x4wd + snowmobile) > 0,
      "Motorized",
      "Non-Motorized"
    )
  ) |>
  
  # 4. Classify Eastern vs. Western WA based on county
  mutate(region = if_else(county %in% eastern_wa_counties,
                          "Eastern WA", "Western WA")) |>
  
  # 5. Keep only the 6 variables needed for Q3
  select(
    objectid,
    trail_name,
    county,               # geographic grouping unit
    region,               # Eastern WA / Western WA
    is_motorized,         # Motorized / Non-Motorized
    segment_length_miles  # for total-miles-per-county aggregation
  )

cat("\n-- df_q3 (Geographic) --\n")
cat("Rows:", nrow(df_q3), "| Cols:", ncol(df_q3), "\n")
cat("Columns:", paste(names(df_q3), collapse = ", "), "\n")
cat("Counties represented:", n_distinct(df_q3$county), "\n")
tabyl(df_q3, region, is_motorized) |> print()
```


```{r q4}
# =============================================================================
# SUB-DATASET 4 — TIME / TREND
# Question: How has trail construction volume changed over time (by decade),
#           and which trail types or agencies drove recent growth?
#
# Variables:
#   objectid             — unique row ID
#   trail_name           — for reference
#   year_constructed     — raw year (continuous, for annual trend line)
#   decade               — derived: floored to decade (1990, 2000, 2010 ...)
#   era                  — derived: labeled period factor (Pre-1970, 1990s, etc.)
#   trail_type_cat       — derived: broad trail type group
#   agency_clean         — derived: top agencies kept; rare ones -> "Other Agency"
#   segment_length_miles — for total miles constructed per period
# =============================================================================

df_q4 <- trails_base |>
  
  # 1. Keep only rows with a recorded construction year
  #    Note: ~22,297 of 22,338 rows are NA here — this sub-dataset will be small
  filter(!is.na(year_constructed)) |>
  
  # 2. Remove implausible years (dataset spans 1900-2023 in raw summary)
  filter(year_constructed >= 1900, year_constructed <= 2025) |>
  
  # 3. Remove invalid segment lengths
  filter(!is.na(segment_length_miles),
         segment_length_miles > 0,
         segment_length_miles <= 50) |>
  
  # 4. Derive decade and era period labels
  mutate(
    decade = (year_constructed %/% 10) * 10,
    era    = case_when(
      year_constructed < 1970 ~ "Pre-1970",
      year_constructed < 1990 ~ "1970s-80s",
      year_constructed < 2000 ~ "1990s",
      year_constructed < 2010 ~ "2000s",
      year_constructed < 2020 ~ "2010s",
      TRUE                    ~ "2020s"
    ) |> factor(levels = c("Pre-1970", "1970s-80s", "1990s",
                           "2000s", "2010s", "2020s"))
  ) |>
  
  # 5. Collapse trail_type into trail_type_cat (7 broad groups)
  mutate(trail_type_cat = case_when(
    str_detect(str_to_lower(trail_type), "hik|foot|pedestrian|walk") ~ "Hiking/Pedestrian",
    str_detect(str_to_lower(trail_type), "multi|shared|paved")       ~ "Multi-Use Paved",
    str_detect(str_to_lower(trail_type), "mtn|mountain bike|bike")   ~ "Mountain Bike",
    str_detect(str_to_lower(trail_type), "motoriz|ohv|atv|ohm")      ~ "Motorized OHV",
    str_detect(str_to_lower(trail_type), "equestrian|horse|pack")    ~ "Equestrian",
    str_detect(str_to_lower(trail_type), "winter|snow|ski")          ~ "Winter",
    str_detect(str_to_lower(trail_type), "water")                    ~ "Water",
    TRUE                                                              ~ "Other/Unknown"
  )) |>
  
  # 6. Collapse rare management agencies into "Other Agency"
  #    Threshold: keep agencies with >= 30 segments in this filtered dataset
  #    This preserves the top ~5-8 agencies for a readable trend chart
  add_count(management_agency, name = "n_agency") |>
  mutate(agency_clean = if_else(n_agency >= 30,
                                management_agency,
                                "Other Agency")) |>
  select(-n_agency) |>
  
  # 7. Keep only the 8 variables needed for Q4
  select(
    objectid,
    trail_name,
    year_constructed,      # raw year: for continuous trend line
    decade,                # grouped: bar / area chart by decade
    era,                   # ordered factor: readable axis labels
    trail_type_cat,        # color / facet: what type was being built?
    agency_clean,          # color / facet: who was building?
    segment_length_miles   # for total miles constructed per period
  )

cat("\n-- df_q4 (Time/Trend) --\n")
cat("Rows:", nrow(df_q4), "| Cols:", ncol(df_q4), "\n")
cat("Columns:", paste(names(df_q4), collapse = ", "), "\n")
tabyl(df_q4, era)            |> print()
tabyl(df_q4, trail_type_cat) |> print()
tabyl(df_q4, agency_clean)   |> print()
```


```{r}
view(df_q1)
view(df_q2)
view(df_q3)
view(df_q4)
```


```{r}


# ── 4. FINAL SUMMARY ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

cat("\n=== CLEANING SUMMARY ===\n")
cat("Raw rows              :", nrow(trails_raw), "\n")
cat("After base cleaning   :", nrow(trails_base), "\n\n")
cat("df_q1  Comparative    :", nrow(df_q1), "rows x", ncol(df_q1), "vars\n")
cat("df_q2  Correlation    :", nrow(df_q2), "rows x", ncol(df_q2), "vars\n")
cat("df_q3  Geographic     :", nrow(df_q3), "rows x", ncol(df_q3), "vars\n")
cat("df_q4  Time/Trend     :", nrow(df_q4), "rows x", ncol(df_q4), "vars\n")
cat("
Key cleaning decisions:
  * segment_length_miles <= 0 or > 50 dropped (errors / cumulative totals)
  * trail_width = 0 dropped (physically impossible)
  * year_constructed outside 1900-2025 dropped (implausible)
  * Blank / 'Unknown' / 'N/A' strings normalized to NA (all char cols)
  * trail_status filtered to active / existing trails only
  * Use-type Yes/No columns binarized (NA treated as 0 = not permitted)
  * County names: 'County' suffix stripped, title-cased, whitespace trimmed
  * Surface / use / trail_type collapsed to broad readable categories
  * Agencies with < 30 segments in Q4 collapsed to 'Other Agency'
")
```


# =============================================================================
# EDA Visualizations
# =============================================================================

```{r}
# library
library(scales)      # label_comma(), percent_format()
library(ggtext)      # element_markdown() for styled axis/title text

# Shared minimal theme used across all four plots
theme_trails <- function() {
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
    theme(
      plot.title      = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14, hjust = 0),
      plot.subtitle   = element_text(size = 10, colour = "grey40", hjust = 0,
                                     margin = margin(b = 10)),
      plot.caption    = element_text(size = 8, colour = "grey55", hjust = 0),
      axis.title      = element_text(size = 10),
      legend.position = "bottom",
      legend.title    = element_text(size = 9),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
    )
}
```


```{r v1}
# =============================================================================
# Q1 — COMPARATIVE
# "How do trail surface types compare across primary use categories in terms
#  of average segment length and trail width?"
#
# Chart choice: Grouped box plot
#   x     = surface_cat  (the comparison groups)
#   y     = segment_length_miles  (continuous outcome)
#   color = surface_cat  (for visual distinction)
#   note  : use_cat is nearly all Hiking/Walking (97.5%) so we annotate
#            rather than facet, to avoid near-empty panels
# =============================================================================

# ── Summarise median length per surface for annotation ───────────────────────
q1_medians <- df_q1 |>
  group_by(surface_cat) |>
  summarise(
    med_length = median(segment_length_miles),
    med_width  = median(trail_width),
    n          = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  arrange(desc(med_length))

# ── Plot ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
plot_q1 <- df_q1 |>
  # Re-order surface_cat by median length (most informative ordering)
  mutate(surface_cat = fct_reorder(surface_cat,
                                   segment_length_miles,
                                   .fun = median)) |>

  ggplot(aes(x = surface_cat,
             y = segment_length_miles,
             fill = surface_cat)) +

  # Box plot: shows median, IQR, and spread
  geom_boxplot(
    outlier.shape  = 16,
    outlier.size   = 0.8,
    outlier.alpha  = 0.4,
    width          = 0.55,
    colour         = "grey30"
  ) +

  # Overlay jittered points so individual segments are visible
  geom_jitter(
    aes(colour = surface_cat),
    width   = 0.15,
    alpha   = 0.25,
    size    = 0.7
  ) +

  # Annotate median value above each box
  stat_summary(
    fun = median,
    geom = "text",
    aes(label = paste0("med=", round(after_stat(y), 2), " mi")),
    vjust = -0.5,
    size  = 2.8,
    colour = "grey20"
  ) +

  # Annotate sample size below x-axis labels
  geom_text(
    data = q1_medians |>
      mutate(surface_cat = fct_reorder(surface_cat, med_length)),
    aes(x = surface_cat, y = -0.5, label = paste0("n=", n)),
    size = 2.6, colour = "grey45", inherit.aes = FALSE
  ) +

  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set2") +
  scale_colour_brewer(palette = "Set2") +
  scale_y_continuous(
    labels = label_number(suffix = " mi"),
    limits = c(-1, 20),   # cap at 20 mi to show detail; outliers still shown
    oob    = scales::squish
  ) +

  labs(
    title    = "Trail Segment Length by Surface Type (Refinement needed)",
    x        = "Surface Category",
    y        = "Segment Length (miles)",
    fill     = "Surface",
    colour   = "Surface"
  ) +

  theme_trails() +
  theme(legend.position = "none")   # colour already encoded on x-axis

print(plot_q1)
ggsave("plot_q1.png", plot_q1, width = 10, height = 10, dpi = 150)
```

```{r v1-1}
# =============================================================================
# Q1 — REFINED COMPARATIVE
#
# Problems in v1:
#   • All medians near 0 (0.04–0.19 mi) — boxes invisible on linear scale
#   • "Other" surface (49% of data) is an uninformative catch-all
#   • Jitter points dominate; box shape unreadable
#
# Fixes:
#   1. Remove "Other" surface category (uninterpretable label)
#   2. Log10 y-axis — spreads the compressed low-end distribution
#   3. Violin plot instead of box — shows the full shape at small scales
#   4. Overlay median dot + IQR bar for a clean summary layer
#   5. Add median label in readable position (not squished at zero)
# =============================================================================
 
q1_refined <- df_q1 |>
  filter(surface_cat != "Other") |>           # remove uninformative category
  filter(segment_length_miles >= 0.01)        # log10 requires > 0; drop micro-segments
 
# Reorder by median for meaningful left-to-right comparison
q1_refined <- q1_refined |>
  mutate(surface_cat = fct_reorder(surface_cat, segment_length_miles, median))
 
# Summary stats for annotation layer
q1_stats <- q1_refined |>
  group_by(surface_cat) |>
  summarise(
    med   = median(segment_length_miles),
    q25   = quantile(segment_length_miles, 0.25),
    q75   = quantile(segment_length_miles, 0.75),
    n     = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  )
 
plot_q1_refined <- ggplot(q1_refined,
                          aes(x = surface_cat, y = segment_length_miles,
                              fill = surface_cat, colour = surface_cat)) +
 
  # Violin: shows the full distribution shape on log scale
  geom_violin(
    alpha     = 0.35,
    linewidth = 0.4,
    trim      = TRUE,
    scale     = "width"   # same visual width per group regardless of n
  ) +
 
  # Median point
  geom_point(
    data = q1_stats,
    aes(x = surface_cat, y = med),
    size = 3, shape = 21,
    fill = "white", colour = "grey20",
    stroke = 1.2,
    inherit.aes = FALSE
  ) +
 
  # IQR range bar
  geom_linerange(
    data = q1_stats,
    aes(x = surface_cat, ymin = q25, ymax = q75),
    linewidth = 1.2, colour = "grey20",
    inherit.aes = FALSE
  ) +
 
  # Median value label — positioned to the right of each violin
  geom_text(
    data = q1_stats,
    aes(x = surface_cat, y = med,
        label = paste0("med=", round(med, 2), " mi\nn=", comma(n))),
    hjust   = -0.15,
    vjust   = 0.5,
    size    = 2.9,
    colour  = "grey25",
    inherit.aes = FALSE
  ) +
 
  # Log scale: reveals the spread that was invisible on linear scale
  scale_y_log10(
    breaks = c(0.01, 0.05, 0.1, 0.5, 1, 5, 10, 50),
    labels = label_number(suffix = " mi", accuracy = 0.01)
  ) +
 
  scale_fill_brewer(palette   = "Set2") +
  scale_colour_brewer(palette = "Set2") +
 
  coord_flip() +   # horizontal violins are easier to read with text labels
 
  labs(
    title    = "Segment Length Distribution by Surface Type",
    subtitle = "Log scale reveals variation invisible on linear axis.",
    x        = NULL,
    y        = "Segment Length (miles, log scale)"
  ) +
 
  theme_trails() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5),
    legend.position    = "none",
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank()
  )
 
print(plot_q1_refined)
ggsave("plot_q1_refined.png", plot_q1_refined, width = 9, height = 5.5, dpi = 150)
```


```{r v2}
# =============================================================================
# Q2 — CORRELATION
# "Does trail width correlate with the number of permitted use types?"
#
# Chart choice: Scatter plot + linear smoother + marginal rug
#   x     = trail_width   (continuous predictor)
#   y     = num_uses      (discrete count, jittered)
#   color = motor_type    (Motorized Only / Non-Motorized Only / Mixed)
#
# Data note from cleaning output:
#   num_uses max = 3, median = 1 — distribution is very right-skewed
#   motor_type is almost entirely "Non-Motorized Only" (2515 / 2516)
#   → we keep motor_type as colour but note the imbalance in subtitle
# =============================================================================

plot_q2 <- df_q2 |>
  # Jitter y slightly so overlapping points separate
  ggplot(aes(x = trail_width,
             y = num_uses,
             colour = motor_type)) +

  # Points — jitter vertically since num_uses is discrete (1, 2, 3)
  geom_jitter(
    height = 0.15,
    width  = 0.1,
    alpha  = 0.35,
    size   = 1.2
  ) +

  # Linear smoother per motor_type group
  geom_smooth(
    method  = "lm",
    formula = y ~ x,
    se      = TRUE,
    linewidth = 0.9,
    alpha   = 0.15
  ) +

  # Rug plot on x-axis to show trail width density
  geom_rug(
    sides  = "b",
    alpha  = 0.15,
    colour = "grey40"
  ) +

  # Pearson correlation annotation (overall)
  annotate(
    "text",
    x     = 28, y = 3.3,
    label = paste0("r = ",
                   round(cor(df_q2$trail_width, df_q2$num_uses,
                             use = "complete.obs"), 3)),
    size  = 3.5,
    colour = "grey20",
    hjust = 1
  ) +

  scale_colour_manual(
    values = c(
      "Non-Motorized Only" = "#2196F3",
      "Motorized Only"     = "#F44336",
      "Mixed"              = "#FF9800",
      "Unclassified"       = "grey60"
    )
  ) +

  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = seq(0, 35, by = 5),
    labels = label_number(suffix = " ft")
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    breaks = 1:3,
    labels = c("1 use", "2 uses", "3 uses")
  ) +

  labs(
    title    = "Trail Width vs. Number of Permitted Uses (Refinement Needed",
    x        = "Trail Width (feet)",
    y        = "Number of Permitted Use Types",
    colour   = "Motor Type"
  ) +

  theme_trails()

print(plot_q2)
ggsave("plot_q2.png", plot_q2, width = 10, height = 10, dpi = 150)
```
```{r v2-1}
# =============================================================================
# Q2 — REFINED CORRELATION
#
# Problems in v2:
#   • r = 0.053 — essentially no linear correlation; scatter plot is flat
#   • num_uses only has values 1, 2, 3 — scatter with 3 rows is uninformative
#   • 99.96% of segments are Non-Motorized — motor_type colour is redundant
#
# Fixes:
#   1. Bin trail_width into interpretable groups (narrow / medium / wide)
#   2. Grouped bar: % of segments at each num_uses level per width bin
#      → shows whether wider trails skew toward multi-use even without linear r
#   3. Annotate actual counts on bars so readers see the n behind percentages
#   4. Facet by num_uses (1, 2, 3) so each panel is readable
# =============================================================================
 
q2_refined <- df_q2 |>
  mutate(
    width_bin = case_when(
      trail_width <= 4  ~ "Narrow\n(1–4 ft)",
      trail_width <= 8  ~ "Medium\n(5–8 ft)",
      trail_width <= 12 ~ "Standard\n(9–12 ft)",
      trail_width <= 20 ~ "Wide\n(13–20 ft)",
      TRUE              ~ "Very Wide\n(>20 ft)"
    ) |>
      factor(levels = c("Narrow\n(1–4 ft)", "Medium\n(5–8 ft)",
                        "Standard\n(9–12 ft)", "Wide\n(13–20 ft)",
                        "Very Wide\n(>20 ft)"))
  )
 
# Compute % of segments at each num_uses within each width_bin
q2_pct <- q2_refined |>
  count(width_bin, num_uses) |>
  group_by(width_bin) |>
  mutate(
    pct       = n / sum(n),
    bin_total = sum(n)
  ) |>
  ungroup() |>
  mutate(num_uses_label = paste0(num_uses, ifelse(num_uses == 1, " use", " uses")))
 
plot_q2_refined <- q2_pct |>
  ggplot(aes(x = width_bin, y = pct, fill = factor(num_uses))) +
 
  geom_col(position = "dodge", width = 0.7) +
 
  # % label on each bar
  geom_text(
    aes(label = percent(pct, accuracy = 1)),
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.7),
    vjust    = -0.4,
    size     = 2.7,
    colour   = "grey20"
  ) +
 
  # Annotate total n per width bin along the x-axis
  geom_text(
    data = q2_pct |> distinct(width_bin, bin_total),
    aes(x = width_bin, y = -0.04,
        label = paste0("n=", comma(bin_total))),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    size    = 2.6,
    colour  = "grey45"
  ) +
 
  scale_y_continuous(
    labels = percent_format(accuracy = 1),
    limits = c(-0.06, 1.05),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.05))
  ) +
 
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c("1" = "#AEC6CF", "2" = "#6B9EB5", "3" = "#2A5D7A"),
    labels = c("1" = "1 use", "2" = "2 uses", "3" = "3 uses")
  ) +
 
  labs(
    title    = "Trail Width vs. Number of Permitted Uses",
    subtitle = "n = 2,516",
    x        = "Trail Width Category",
    y        = "Share of Segments",
    fill     = "Permitted Uses"
  ) +
 
  theme_trails() +
  theme( 
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank()
    )
 
print(plot_q2_refined)
ggsave("plot_q2_refined.png", plot_q2_refined, width = 9, height = 5.5, dpi = 150)
```


```{r v3}
# =============================================================================
# Q3 — GEOGRAPHIC
# "Which WA counties have the highest density of motorized vs. non-motorized
#  trails, and are motorized trails concentrated in Eastern WA?"
#
# Chart choice: Horizontal stacked bar — top 20 counties by total miles
#   x     = total trail miles (aggregated from segment_length_miles)
#   y     = county (ordered by total miles, top 20)
#   fill  = is_motorized (Motorized / Non-Motorized)
#   facet = region (Eastern WA / Western WA) — tests the east/west hypothesis
#
# Data note: 90 counties; showing top 20 by total miles keeps chart readable
# =============================================================================

# ── Aggregate to county level ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
q3_county <- df_q3 |>
  group_by(county, region, is_motorized) |>
  summarise(total_miles = sum(segment_length_miles, na.rm = TRUE),
            n_segments  = n(),
            .groups = "drop") |>
  # Compute total miles per county (for ordering)
  group_by(county) |>
  mutate(county_total = sum(total_miles)) |>
  ungroup()

# ── Keep top 20 counties by total trail miles ─────────────────────────────────
top20_counties <- q3_county |>
  distinct(county, county_total) |>
  slice_max(county_total, n = 20) |>
  pull(county)

q3_plot_data <- q3_county |>
  filter(county %in% top20_counties) |>
  mutate(county = fct_reorder(county, county_total))

# ── Proportion of motorized per region (for subtitle) ────────────────────────
q3_pct <- df_q3 |>
  group_by(region, is_motorized) |>
  summarise(n = n(), .groups = "drop") |>
  group_by(region) |>
  mutate(pct = n / sum(n)) |>
  filter(is_motorized == "Motorized")

# ── Plot ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
plot_q3 <- q3_plot_data |>
  ggplot(aes(x = total_miles,
             y = county,
             fill = is_motorized)) +

  geom_col(position = "stack", width = 0.7) +

  # Total miles label at end of each bar
  geom_text(
    data = q3_plot_data |>
      group_by(county, county_total) |>
      summarise(.groups = "drop"),
    aes(x = county_total, y = county,
        label = paste0(round(county_total, 0), " mi")),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    hjust = -0.1, size = 2.6, colour = "grey30"
  ) +

  # Facet by Eastern vs Western WA — directly tests the hypothesis
  facet_wrap(~ region, scales = "free_y", ncol = 1) +

  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c("Motorized" = "#E07B39", "Non-Motorized" = "#4A90A4"),
    labels = c("Motorized" = "Motorized", "Non-Motorized" = "Non-Motorized")
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.18)),
    labels = label_number(suffix = " mi")
  ) +

  labs(
    title    = "Trail Miles by County: Motorized vs. Non-Motorized(Refinement needed",
    x        = "Total Trail Miles",
    y        = NULL,
    fill     = "Trail Type"
  ) +

  theme_trails() +
  theme(
    strip.text       = element_text(face = "bold", size = 11),
    strip.background = element_rect(fill = "grey93", colour = NA),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank()
  )

print(plot_q3)
ggsave("plot_q3.png", plot_q3, width = 10, height = 10, dpi = 150)
```

```{r v3-1}
# =============================================================================
# Q3 — REFINED GEOGRAPHIC
#
# Problems in v3:
#   • Counties duplicated across both facets (Eastern counties appear in
#     Western panel and vice versa) — fct_reorder was global, not per-region
#   • Top 20 was global; some Eastern counties appeared in Western facet
#   • Labels overlapped badly
#   • x-axis labels had spaces in thousands (1 000 mi)
#
# Fixes:
#   1. Take top 10 counties WITHIN each region separately → no duplication
#   2. Use facet_grid with independent y scales so county lists don't bleed
#   3. fct_reorder within region using reorder_within() + scale_y_reordered()
#   4. Clean comma-format x-axis labels
#   5. Move mile labels inside the bar for bars > 100 mi (avoids overflow)
# =============================================================================
 
# ── Aggregate to county × region × is_motorized ──────────────────────────────
q3_county <- df_q3 |>
  group_by(county, region, is_motorized) |>
  summarise(total_miles = sum(segment_length_miles, na.rm = TRUE),
            .groups = "drop") |>
  group_by(county, region) |>
  mutate(county_total = sum(total_miles)) |>
  ungroup()
 
# ── Top 10 counties WITHIN each region (no cross-region contamination) ────────
top_eastern <- q3_county |>
  filter(region == "Eastern WA") |>
  distinct(county, county_total) |>
  slice_max(county_total, n = 10) |>
  pull(county)
 
top_western <- q3_county |>
  filter(region == "Western WA") |>
  distinct(county, county_total) |>
  slice_max(county_total, n = 10) |>
  pull(county)
 
q3_plot_data <- q3_county |>
  filter(
    (region == "Eastern WA" & county %in% top_eastern) |
    (region == "Western WA" & county %in% top_western)
  )
 
# Reorder counties within each region independently — base R approach
# (avoids needing tidytext::reorder_within)
# Strategy: build a factor level order by sorting each region separately,
# then concatenate Eastern levels first, Western levels second.
# facet_wrap(scales = "free_y") will only show the relevant subset per panel.
 
eastern_order <- q3_plot_data |>
  filter(region == "Eastern WA") |>
  distinct(county, county_total) |>
  arrange(county_total) |>         # ascending so highest is at top of flipped axis
  pull(county)
 
western_order <- q3_plot_data |>
  filter(region == "Western WA") |>
  distinct(county, county_total) |>
  arrange(county_total) |>
  pull(county)
 
# Combined factor: Eastern levels first, then Western
# Each facet only renders its own subset so the ordering is correct per panel
q3_plot_data <- q3_plot_data |>
  mutate(county = factor(county, levels = c(eastern_order, western_order)))
 
# ── Region-level motorized % for subtitle ────────────────────────────────────
q3_pct <- df_q3 |>
  count(region, is_motorized) |>
  group_by(region) |>
  mutate(pct = n / sum(n)) |>
  filter(is_motorized == "Motorized") |>
  select(region, pct)
 
east_pct <- q3_pct |> filter(region == "Eastern WA") |> pull(pct)
west_pct <- q3_pct |> filter(region == "Western WA") |> pull(pct)
 
# ── Plot ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
plot_q3_refined <- q3_plot_data |>
  ggplot(aes(x = total_miles, y = county, fill = is_motorized)) +
 
  geom_col(position = "stack", width = 0.65) +
 
  # Label: inside bar if wide enough, outside if narrow
  geom_text(
    data = q3_plot_data |>
      group_by(county, region, county_total) |>
      summarise(.groups = "drop"),
    aes(x = county_total, y = county,
        label = paste0(comma(round(county_total, 0)), " mi"),
        hjust = if_else(county_total > 300, -0.08, -0.08)),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    size = 2.7, colour = "grey25"
  ) +
 
  # Independent y scale per facet so each region shows only its own counties
  facet_wrap(~ region, scales = "free_y", ncol = 1) +
 
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c("Motorized" = "#E07B39", "Non-Motorized" = "#4A90A4")
  ) +
 
  scale_x_continuous(
    labels  = label_comma(suffix = " mi"),
    expand  = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.18))
  ) +
 
  labs(
    title    = "Trail Miles by County: Motorized vs. Non-Motorized",
    subtitle = paste0(
      "Top 10 counties per region shown"
    ),
    x        = "Total Trail Miles",
    y        = NULL,
    fill     = "Trail Type"
  ) +
 
  theme_trails() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5),
    strip.text         = element_text(face = "bold", size = 11),
    strip.background   = element_rect(fill = "grey93", colour = NA),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank()
  )
 
print(plot_q3_refined)
ggsave("plot_q3_refined.png", plot_q3_refined, width = 9, height = 9, dpi = 150)
```




```{r v4}
# =============================================================================
# Q4 — TIME / TREND
# "How has trail construction volume changed over time by decade, and which
#  trail types or agencies drove recent growth?"
#
# IMPORTANT data note from cleaning output:
#   df_q4 has only 40 rows (year_constructed is NA for 22,297 / 22,338 rows)
#   trail_type_cat = 100% "Other/Unknown" — trail_type was not recorded
#   agency_clean   = 75% "City of Bonney Lake", 25% "Other Agency"
#   → A standard stacked bar by trail_type is meaningless here
#   → Instead: bar chart of segment COUNT and total MILES by era,
#     coloured by agency_clean, with a clear data-limitation note
# =============================================================================

# ── Aggregate by era × agency ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
q4_summary <- df_q4 |>
  group_by(era, agency_clean) |>
  summarise(
    n_segments  = n(),
    total_miles = sum(segment_length_miles, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# ── Plot — dual panel: segment count (left) and total miles (right) ───────────
# Pivot to long for faceting both metrics in one chart
q4_long <- q4_summary |>
  pivot_longer(cols = c(n_segments, total_miles),
               names_to  = "metric",
               values_to = "value") |>
  mutate(metric = recode(metric,
                         "n_segments"  = "Number of Segments",
                         "total_miles" = "Total Miles Constructed"))

plot_q4 <- q4_long |>
  ggplot(aes(x = era,
             y = value,
             fill = agency_clean)) +

  geom_col(position = "stack", width = 0.6) +

  # Value label on each bar total
  geom_text(
    data = q4_long |>
      group_by(era, metric) |>
      summarise(total = sum(value), .groups = "drop"),
    aes(x = era, y = total, label = round(total, 1)),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    vjust = -0.4, size = 3, colour = "grey25"
  ) +

  facet_wrap(~ metric, scales = "free_y", ncol = 2) +

  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c(
      "City of Bonney Lake" = "#5C85C4",
      "Other Agency"        = "#A8C8A0"
    )
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.15))) +

  labs(
    title    = "Trail Construction Volume by Era",
    x        = "Construction Era",
    y        = NULL,
    fill     = "Management Agency"
  ) +

  theme_trails() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5),
    strip.text       = element_text(face = "bold", size = 10),
    strip.background = element_rect(fill = "grey93", colour = NA),
    axis.text.x      = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1)
  )

print(plot_q4)
ggsave("plot_q4.png", plot_q4, width = 10, height = 10, dpi = 150)
```


# =============================================================================
# Final Question: Beginner Trail Accessibility Score
# =============================================================================

```{r fq}
# Question: Can we construct a county-level Beginner Trail Accessibility Score
# — weighted by non-motorized trail miles, proportion of paved or gravel
# surface, and short segment length — to rank Washington counties and identify
# the most beginner-friendly regions for first-time hikers?
#
# Score Components (equal weight, each scaled 0–1):
#   Component 1 — Non-Motorized Share:
#     proportion of trail miles that are non-motorized per county
#   Component 2 — Gentle Surface Share:
#     proportion of segments on paved or gravel/crushed surface per county
#   Component 3 — Short Segment Share:
#     proportion of segments with length <= 2 miles per county
#
# Final Score = mean(component_1, component_2, component_3), scaled 0–100

# =============================================================================
```

```{r fq1}
library(tidyverse)
library(janitor)
library(scales)

# ── 1. BUILD THE SEGMENT-LEVEL BASE ──────────────────────────────────────────
#
# We join the key variables from df_q1 (surface_cat) and df_q3 (county,
# region, is_motorized) using objectid as the key.
# df_q3 has the broadest coverage (22,003 rows) so we start there
# and left-join surface information from df_q1.
# =============================================================================

df_final <- df_q3 |>

  # Join surface_cat from df_q1 on objectid
  # Not all segments in df_q3 will have a surface_cat (df_q1 required
  # trail_surface and trail_width to be non-missing) — those get NA
  left_join(
    df_q1 |> select(objectid, surface_cat),
    by = "objectid"
  ) |>

  # ── Flag 1: is the segment non-motorized? ─────────────────────────────────
  # Already present in df_q3 as is_motorized ("Motorized" / "Non-Motorized")
  mutate(
    flag_nonmotorized = if_else(is_motorized == "Non-Motorized", 1L, 0L)
  ) |>

  # ── Flag 2: is the surface beginner-gentle? ───────────────────────────────
  # Paved and Gravel/Crushed are the most forgiving for beginners
  # NA surface = unknown → treated as 0 (conservative)
  mutate(
    flag_gentle_surface = case_when(
      surface_cat %in% c("Paved", "Gravel/Crushed") ~ 1L,
      is.na(surface_cat)                            ~ 0L,
      TRUE                                          ~ 0L
    )
  ) |>

  # ── Flag 3: is the segment short (≤ 2 miles)? ────────────────────────────
  # 2 miles chosen as the beginner threshold:
  # matches "Easy" tier in your Education Phase (1–3 miles)
  # and aligns with the AllTrails dataset beginner range
  mutate(
    flag_short = if_else(segment_length_miles <= 2, 1L, 0L)
  )

cat("df_final rows:", nrow(df_final), "\n")
cat("Columns:", paste(names(df_final), collapse = ", "), "\n")
```


```{r fq2}
# ── 2. AGGREGATE TO COUNTY LEVEL ─────────────────────────────────────────────
#
# For each county compute:
#   total_miles        — total trail miles (for context / weighting)
#   n_segments         — number of trail segments
#   nonmotorized_share — proportion of segments that are non-motorized
#   gentle_surface_share — proportion of segments with paved/gravel surface
#   short_share        — proportion of segments <= 2 miles
# =============================================================================

df_score_raw <- df_final |>

  group_by(county, region) |>
  summarise(
    n_segments           = n(),
    total_miles          = sum(segment_length_miles, na.rm = TRUE),
    nonmotorized_share   = mean(flag_nonmotorized,    na.rm = TRUE),
    gentle_surface_share = mean(flag_gentle_surface,  na.rm = TRUE),
    short_share          = mean(flag_short,            na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>

  # Remove counties with fewer than 10 segments — too sparse for a reliable score
  filter(n_segments >= 10)

cat("\nCounties with >= 10 segments:", nrow(df_score_raw), "\n")
```




```{r fq3}

# ── 3. COMPUTE BEGINNER TRAIL ACCESSIBILITY SCORE ─────────────────────────────
#
# Each component is already on a 0–1 scale (it's a proportion).
# Score = mean of the three components, multiplied by 100 for readability.
#
# Weighting rationale:
#   Non-motorized share  — most critical for safety / beginner comfort (weight 0.4)
#   Short segment share  — key effort predictor for beginners (weight 0.4)
#   Gentle surface share — important but data is sparse (weight 0.2)
#
# Weights sum to 1.0.
# =============================================================================

df_score <- df_score_raw |>

  mutate(
    # Weighted composite score (0–100)
    beginner_score = (
      0.40 * nonmotorized_share +
      0.40 * short_share        +
      0.20 * gentle_surface_share
    ) * 100
  ) |>

  # Round components to 2 decimal places for display
  mutate(across(c(nonmotorized_share, gentle_surface_share,
                  short_share, beginner_score), ~ round(., 2))) |>

  # Rank counties overall (1 = most beginner friendly)
  mutate(rank_overall = rank(-beginner_score, ties.method = "min")) |>

  # Rank within each region separately
  group_by(region) |>
  mutate(rank_region = rank(-beginner_score, ties.method = "min")) |>
  ungroup() |>

  arrange(rank_overall)

cat("\n── Beginner Score Summary ──\n")
summary(df_score$beginner_score)

cat("\n── Top 15 Counties Overall ──\n")
df_score |>
  select(rank_overall, county, region, beginner_score,
         nonmotorized_share, gentle_surface_share, short_share,
         total_miles, n_segments) |>
  slice_min(rank_overall, n = 15) |>
  print(n = 15)
```


```{r fq4}
# ── 4. TOP 10 PER REGION ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────

# Helper: reorder county factor within each region for ggplot
# (base R version — no tidytext dependency)
eastern_order_score <- df_score |>
  filter(region == "Eastern WA") |>
  slice_min(rank_region, n = 10) |>
  arrange(beginner_score) |>
  pull(county)
 
western_order_score <- df_score |>
  filter(region == "Western WA") |>
  slice_min(rank_region, n = 10) |>
  arrange(beginner_score) |>
  pull(county)
 
df_score_top <- df_score |>
  group_by(region) |>
  slice_min(rank_region, n = 10) |>
  ungroup() |>
  mutate(county = factor(county,
                         levels = c(eastern_order_score,
                                    western_order_score)))
 
```


```{r fq5}
# ── 5. VISUALIZATION — BEGINNER SCORE BY COUNTY ───────────────────────────────
#
# Chart: Horizontal bar chart, faceted by region (Eastern / Western WA)
#   x    = beginner_score (0–100)
#   y    = county (ordered by score within region)
#   fill = beginner_score (continuous color scale: low = muted, high = green)
#   Top 10 per region shown
# =============================================================================

theme_trails <- function() {
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
    theme(
      plot.title       = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14, hjust = 0.5),
      plot.subtitle    = element_text(size = 10, colour = "grey40", hjust = 0.5,
                                      margin = margin(b = 10)),
      plot.caption     = element_text(size = 8, colour = "grey55", hjust = 0),
      axis.title       = element_text(size = 10),
      legend.position  = "bottom",
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
    )
}

plot_score <- df_score_top |>
  ggplot(aes(x = beginner_score, y = county, fill = beginner_score)) +

  geom_col(width = 0.65) +

  # Score label at end of bar
  geom_text(
    aes(label = paste0(beginner_score)),
    hjust  = -0.15,
    size   = 2.8,
    colour = "grey25"
  ) +

  # Component breakdown annotation inside bar
  geom_text(
    aes(label = paste0(
      "NM:", percent(nonmotorized_share, accuracy = 1),
      " Sfc:", percent(gentle_surface_share, accuracy = 1),
      " Sh:", percent(short_share, accuracy = 1)
    )),
    x      = 1,
    hjust  = 0,
    size   = 2.3,
    colour = "white"
  ) +

  facet_wrap(~ region, scales = "free_y", ncol = 1) +

  scale_fill_gradient(
    low    = "#A8C8A0",   # muted green = lower score
    high   = "#1B5E20",   # deep green = highest score
    name   = "Score",
    limits = c(0, 100)
  ) +

  scale_x_continuous(
    limits = c(0, 110),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0))
  ) +

  labs(
    title    = "Beginner Trail Accessibility Score by County",
    subtitle = paste0(
      "Score (0–100) weighted: Non-Motorized(NM) 40% + Short Segment(Sh) 40% + Gentle Surface(Sfc) 20%\n",
      "Top 10 counties per region  |  Only counties with ≥ 10 trail segments included"
    ),
    x        = "Beginner Trail Accessibility Score",
    y        = NULL,
    caption  = "Short = ≤ 2 mi  |  Gentle surface = Paved or Gravel/Crushed"
  ) +

  theme_trails() +
  theme(
    strip.text         = element_text(face = "bold", size = 11),
    strip.background   = element_rect(fill = "grey93", colour = NA),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    legend.position    = "none"
  )

print(plot_score)
ggsave("plot_beginner_score.png", plot_score, width = 10, height = 10, dpi = 150)
```
